finding: Phase-1 discovery (phase2-handoff-notes revision note, post-Step-9 gate simulation, 2026-06-10): a panel-suffixed token like 'FIG. 4B' is invisible to the figure-token regex \bfig(?:ure|\.|-)?\s*0*(\d+)\b because the trailing letter removes the word boundary after the digits. A draft using only 'FIG. 4B' would falsely orphan figure 4 (spurious FIGUSE-001 hard fail) and miss FIGREF resolution. This run worked around it by mandating the parseable form 'FIG. 4 (panel B)'. Latent for any patent with lettered sub-figures.
recommendation: Candidate gate-strengthening if it recurs: extend the figure-token regex to accept an optional panel-letter suffix, shipped with a test_gates.py case; until then keep the handoff-notes token-form workaround.

root_cause_artifact: thesis-architect/references/invention-summary-schema.md claim layer + phase2-handoff-notes trap list (claim-internal ordering semantics not pre-chewed)
finding: Claim-order misattribution: SS3 intro/narration (+ SS1 P2 inconsistency) presented the description's preferred wipe-first order as locked by claim 1, while claim 1 locks only the tail partial order (oil after first blade starts moving, second wipe after oil [8001]). Grounding hard-gate breach, invisible to all deterministic gates. Phase-1 traps covered the adjacent classes (claim-8 dependency, design-goal-vs-claim) but not claim-1 internal ordering semantics. [resolved in iter 2: order re-attributed to the description via [0021]/[0038]/[9015], claim lock restated precisely]
recommendation: Add a claim-internal-semantics trap class to the Phase-1 trap checklist: for each independent claim, state exactly which orderings/conditions the claim locks vs leaves open, so Compose narrates embodiment ordering as the description's preference, never as claim-mandated.

finding: Cover universalization (SS1 P1 / SS2 P1 / SS2 title): prose universalized 'the industry sprays its covers' although the fact-check-log (dlhbowles-spray-nozzle-patents) shows the nozzles can also spray a bare objective-lens surface; the verified invariant is non-contact cleaning, not cover-as-universal. [resolved in iter 2: rescoped to the supported non-contact invariant; SS2 title kept under explicit allowance]
recommendation: Tie external-fact prose scope to the fact-check-log entry's literal scope; assert a universal only when the log states one.

root_cause_artifact: essay-en-composer/references/section-blueprint.md (no paragraph word-length band for mobile rendering)
finding: Mobile wall-of-text: SS5 P3 124w, SS5 P5 120w, SS4 P1 115w exceed the words/12 > 8-line mobile heuristic. gate_structure STRUCT-001 counts sentences (max 8), not words, so 4-6-sentence walls of long sentences pass the warn-only gate unflagged. [resolved in iter 2: all three split at the recommended seams, confirmed by word arithmetic]
recommendation: Candidate gate-strengthening: add a word-count warn heuristic (~>110w per paragraph) to gate_structure; and/or add a mobile paragraph-length band to section-blueprint so Compose self-limits.

finding: Antithesis contrast-pair cluster (~6 instances, e.g. 'a blink, not an outage'): density reads as an AI-tell pattern; load-bearing pairs (two bold anchors + closing pair) legitimate, incidental ones not. [resolved in iter 2: incidental instance rephrased to 'For the camera, the event is a blink.', cluster reduced to ~5]
recommendation: If contrast-pair clustering recurs across essays, add judged density guidance to anti-ai-writing.md (voice fence respected: routes to anti-ai canon, not voice-profile).

root_cause_artifact: _shared/references/anti-ai-writing.md (emoji allowance conditioned on a canon pattern not visible inside the Phase-3 fence)
finding: Essay-end thinking-emoji attached to a blended closing (aphoristic landing + forward pointer): the allowance admits the emoji only for the closing-open-question pattern, but the voice-fenced editor cannot read voice-canon entries to verify the blended landing qualifies. Fence-design tension: the rule's qualifying condition lives outside the editor's fence. [carried to iter 2 as ED2-01]
recommendation: Make the emoji allowance fence-decidable: restate the qualifying condition mechanically in anti-ai-writing.md or deliverable-voice-rules.md (both fence-visible), or have Phase-2 thesis-trace carry a canon-conformance declaration the editor can trust. Never re-expose voice-profile or canon entries to Phase 3.

finding: Word-level doubling: 'renewed/renewable' repeated between the SS3 bold anchor and the next paragraph. [resolved in iter 2: 'renewable' cut, bold anchor owns the renewal beat alone]
recommendation: A bold anchor owns its beat; adjacent prose must not re-spend the same key word.

finding: Header caption vs SS3 P1 near-verbatim echo ('packaged around one exposed optic'). [resolved in iter 2: body varied to 'every part of it serving one exposed optic'; remaining conceptual refrain reads as deliberate]
recommendation: Vary caption-derived phrasing in body prose; keep refrains conceptual, not lexical.

finding: Embodiment-hedge compression cluster (SS2 P3, SS3 P5, SS3 P7): may/can hedges compressed in paraphrase; judged intentional restatement and accepted as-is (values byte-accurate to [0020]/[0021]/[0012], no derived arithmetic, hedges kept elsewhere). [closed in iter 2 re-verification]
recommendation: Acceptable while values stay byte-accurate and hedges survive nearby; watch for drift toward unhedged universals across essays.

finding: Bold emphasis added inside the claim-1 blockquote without an '(emphasis added)' notation; quote otherwise byte-matches q-8001-1. [resolved in iter 2: '(emphasis added)' appended to the attribution line]
recommendation: Citation format should require '(emphasis added)' whenever composer styling is introduced inside verbatim patent text.

finding: Inline source-pointer style inconsistency: one pointer written '(dlhBowles patents, in Sources)' against the bare-label style used elsewhere. [resolved in iter 2: all five inline pointers uniform bare-label style]
recommendation: Specify a single inline source-pointer style in x-articles-format-en.md so consistency does not depend on editor catch.

root_cause_artifact: _shared/references/anti-ai-writing.md (emoji allowance conditioned on a canon pattern not visible inside the Phase-3 fence)
finding: ED2-01 (carried from iter 1, same instance): essay-end thinking-emoji on a blended closing; the v2 closing run does carry an explicit open question two paragraphs up, so the rule is satisfied on its face, but the final paragraph lands on the aphorism and the voice-fenced review still cannot verify the cited canon entries (closing-open-question-tesla-yield-rate).
recommendation: SETI verify against the canon (closing-open-question entry + the aphoristic entry's essay-end marker): keep if the blended landing qualifies, otherwise drop the emoji; no text change needed either way.

root_cause_artifact: editorial-review pass-5 split recommendations vs deliverable-voice-rules 3-7 sentence band (two paragraph criteria not jointly specified)
finding: ED2-02 (new, revision-induced): the iter-1 mandated splits created two 2-sentence paragraphs in the closing run (44w B1 beat, 58w ships-or-not beat) and left SS3 P2 at 2 sentences, under the essay 3-7 sentence band; reads as deliberate cadence acceleration and benefits mobile rendering; accepted as-is. Fixing the pass-5 mobile-wall medium produced a pass-1 band underrun: the 8-line mobile ceiling and the 3-7 sentence band conflict at the margins.
recommendation: Accept as-is; if strict band compliance is wanted, merging re-approaches the 8-line mobile ceiling (~124w). The two paragraph-length criteria need joint specification so revision guidance cannot satisfy one by breaking the other.

finding: ED2-03 (residue of resolved iter-1 medium): cover universalization survives in two compressed positions, the SS2 title 'Spray the Cover, Not the Lens' (kept under iter-1's explicit allowance) and the closing aphorism pair 'The industry sprays its covers.'; body now carries the precise non-contact invariant twice, so the aphorisms read as deliberate compression backed by precise prose.
recommendation: Optional title/aphorism-level precision ('Spray It, Never Touch It'; 'The industry cleans at arm's length.'); otherwise accept as deliberate compression.

root_cause_artifact: essay-en-composer/references/section-blueprint.md (no paragraph word-length band for mobile rendering)
finding: ED2-04 (carried marginals): SS3 P8 107w (~8.9 lines; quote-integrated demotion applies) and SS4 P4 99w (~8.2 lines at the conservative 12 w/line divisor) sit at the mobile heuristic edge; neither is a wall in the iter-1 medium sense; no other paragraph exceeds the heuristic.

root_cause_artifact: thesis-architect/references/invention-summary-schema.md claim layer + phase2-handoff-notes trap wording (trap 10 itself called the [8002]-[8005] limitations 'such floors')
finding: F1: claim 5's pinned Ms point ("martensite formation begins at about 50° C." [8005]) characterized as a 'floor' in §3 and §4 — a bound the claim does not state. Grounding hard-gate breach, invisible to all deterministic gates; sole driver of the round-1 revise-required and a full revision round. Root traces UPSTREAM to Phase 1's own phase2-handoff-notes trap 10, which grouped [8005] under 'such floors'. Same class as run-1's high (claim-1 embodiment order attributed to the claim): the invention-summary claim layer does not state per-claim what is locked vs open vs pinned. [resolved in v2: re-characterized as a pinned transformation point in both locations; verbatim quote untouched]
recommendation: Round-2 editor, carried for retro: 'phase2-handoff-notes trap 10 still calls the [8002]-[8005] limitations such floors — propose fixing the trap wording upstream so the next run does not re-inherit the drift.' Add a per-claim locked/open/pinned scope map to the Phase-1 invention-summary claim layer and restate it in trap form in the handoff notes.

finding: F2: §1 EOS scope overreach — prose stretched the logged fact eos-ms1-18ni300-aging ('typical applications include injection-molding tools and inserts') toward universality ('sold for exactly this kind of work'). Same class as run-1's dlhBowles cover universalization: external-fact prose scope exceeding the fact-check-log entry's literal scope. [resolved in v2: application scope named explicitly; 'among them' supported by the source's 'include']
recommendation: Run-1 recommendation recurs cross-essay: tie external-fact prose scope to the fact-check-log entry's literal scope; assert a universal only when the log states one.

root_cause_artifact: essay-en-composer/references/figure-rendering.md (per-panel condition attribution when narrating multi-panel figures)
finding: F3: FIG. 7 (panel B) protocol ambiguity — prose allowed panel A's 510/575 C protocol to be inferred onto B1, whose own treatment is different (10 h aging at 450 C, [0077]). [resolved in v2: B1's own conditions stated explicitly, byte-faithful to [0077]]
recommendation: When narrating one panel of a multi-panel figure, state that panel's own test conditions; never let an adjacent panel's protocol carry over by ambiguity.

finding: F4: unsourced 'this morning' — time-of-day detail beyond the sourced publication date. [resolved in v2: 'published today' (sourced date only); the doubled 'today' removed]
recommendation: Date-level facts only; no invented time-of-day color.

finding: F5: 'would hold a patent on the excluded middle' — ownership-of-the-quadrant read plus the 'patent' noun against the A1 pending posture (trap 1 otherwise honored; A1 sweep clean both rounds after fix). [resolved in v2: 'would hold a claim that sits on the excluded middle of die-shop metallurgy'; conditional grant framing kept]
recommendation: Conditional-grant prose must stay claim-shaped, never ownership-shaped, while the application is pending.

finding: F6: claim-21 scope qualifier dropped in paraphrase — 'at least a portion of the conformal cooling channel' compressed to full conformity. Same family as run-1's embodiment-hedge compression (qualifying language lost in paraphrase), but load-bearing this time (claim scope). [resolved in v2: partial-conformity qualifier restored: 'at least part of whose channel']
recommendation: Qualifiers on claim language are scope; paraphrase may compress style, never scope.

root_cause_artifact: _shared/references/anti-ai-writing.md (emoji allowance conditioned on a canon pattern not visible inside the Phase-3 fence)
finding: F7: 🤔 hosted on a declarative aphoristic landing — canon-sanctioned (circuit-contract) but the voice-fenced editor cannot read canon entries to verify, so the allowance is undecidable inside the fence. Escalation of run-1's carried low (same artifact, same gap) to medium. [resolved in v2: composer re-hosted the emoji on a genuine open question ('Can a die keep what the printer draws? 🤔') so anti-ai-writing's rule alone decides, no canon access needed; composer precedent note endorsed by the round-2 editor: prefer interrogative 🤔 hosts because declarative-aphorism + 🤔 is not verifiable under the Phase-3 fence]
recommendation: Make the emoji allowance fence-decidable in anti-ai-writing.md: require the hosting sentence itself be the open question (interrogative). Voice fence respected: routes to anti-ai canon, never voice-profile.

finding: F8: acceptable-repetition awareness flag — floors restated across lead fragment quote / §3 blockquote / FIG. 1 caption / closing synthesis; posture beat x3. Held: no action was required, and v2 added no fourth layer of either.
recommendation: Keep sanctioned repetition layers enumerated so revision does not add a new one unnoticed.

finding: F9: intensifier tic — 'exactly' x4. [resolved in v2: reduced to 2; §4 annealing-literature and §6 question instances kept]
recommendation: Sweep top-frequency intensifiers before handoff; keep only motif-bearing instances.

finding: F10: martensite unglossed at first use + FIG. 3 sweep jargon density, against the declared non-metallurgist audience (phase2-handoff-notes calibration: alloy vocabulary introduced through what it does to the part). [resolved in v2: functional gloss 'the hard structure steel snaps into when it cools fast'; sweep eased to three plain-language items, faithful to [0074]/FIG. 3 panels C-D as Phase 1 describes them; trap-10 'the document states the property and stops' retained]
recommendation: Gloss each load-bearing term functionally at first use; ease multi-clause technical sweeps to plain-language items without inventing mechanism.

URL" with checkable titles; authorless Energies entry kept deliberately — author list not reliably confirmable, and inventing or copying unverified names would be a fact introduction; the MDPI title's published em-dash normalized to a colon, disclosed in thesis-trace]

root_cause_artifact: editorial-review pass-5 split recommendations vs deliverable-voice-rules 3-7 sentence band (two paragraph criteria not jointly specified)
finding: Round 2: the F11 splits created paragraphs below the 3-7-sentence band (§1p3 at 2 sentences; two consecutive single-sentence §3 enumerations, 55w and 69w). Exact recurrence of run-1 ED2-02: fixing the pass-5 mobile-wall produced a pass-1 band underrun — the 8-line ceiling and the 3-7 band still conflict at the margins.

root_cause_artifact: essay-en-composer/references/section-blueprint.md (no paragraph word-length band for mobile rendering)
finding: Round 2: adjudication of the composer's three declared stands on retained 96w+ paragraphs — §1p1 114w (divisor-dependent; splitting would push the thesis out of lead paragraph 2), FIG. 3 paragraph 99w (grew by the editor's own F10 gloss; quote-integrated demotion applies), §6 gigacasting 111w (untouched per round-1's own instruction; in-band, one cohesive idea). Second consecutive run constrained by the lettered-panel regex blindspot: this patent's figures carry 16 lettered panel assets, and phase2-handoff-notes trap 3 again had to mandate the parseable form 'FIG. N (panel X)' and ban 'FIG. 2A'-style sole references so no figure would be falsely orphaned. The defect did not manifest (avoidance-by-convention worked: the round-2 edit-log confirms FIG tokens {1,2,3,5,6,7,8} all present and no letter-suffixed tokens), but the mitigation cost recurs per-run and one unconverted token would spuriously hard-fail FIGUSE-001.

Extend FIG_RE/FIGREF_RE with an optional panel-letter suffix, shipped with test_gates.py cases (proposal on file); the prose '(panel X)' convention stays stylistically preferred.

root_cause_artifact: essay-en-composer/references/x-articles-format-en.md + strip-pipeline.md (no stated policy for em-dashes inside quoted bibliographic titles vs EMDASH-001)
finding: Accepted normalization on record (round-2 cross-check): the Energies paper's published title contains an em-dash; rendering it verbatim in # Sources would trip the zero-em-dash hard rule, so the entry normalized it to a colon, disclosed in thesis-trace, traceability preserved via venue/volume/URL. Improvised policy — no reference states how to handle source-verbatim em-dashes inside Sources titles.
recommendation: Editor: 'Retro may consider a reference note for quoted-title em-dash handling so future runs need not improvise.'

root_cause_artifact: essay-en-composer/references/citation-format.md (claim-language vs spec-paragraph attribution convention) + upstream thesis-trace.md paragraph_anchors_used inheritance
finding: Citation-anchor mislabel: three verbatim CLAIM quotes (q-clm1-1, q-clm1-2 in §2; q-clm17-1 in §3) were tagged with a [0144] bracket — a SPECIFICATION paragraph anchor — so legal claim language was cited as if it came from a spec paragraph, collapsing the claims-vs-specification distinction the investor diligence argument turns on. Quote TEXT was byte-exact (verbatim integrity never at risk); only the attribution LABEL was wrong. The mis-tag was inherited from thesis-trace.md / the upstream anchor map. Sole driver of the iter-1 revise-recommended and one full revision round, invisible to all six deterministic gates (the [0144] bracket is a well-formed 4-digit anchor that resolves in-index, so gate_anchors passes). [resolved in iter 2: brackets removed, claim quotes re-attributed by claim in prose ('Claim 1 is a method...', 'claim 17 covers...'); the sole surviving [0144] is the legit deployment-mechanism spec paraphrase q-0144-1; thesis-trace.md anchor map corrected upstream to state claim quotes are 'attributed by claim in prose, not by a [dddd] spec bracket'.] First-seen class: distinct from claim-scope-misattribution (that is claim SEMANTICS — what the claim locks/pins; this is claim CITATION NOTATION — claim text carrying a spec [dddd] bracket) and adjacent to but distinct from quote-notation-gap (that was missing '(emphasis added)').
recommendation: Composer citation convention should state explicitly: verbatim CLAIM language is attributed by claim number in prose ('claim 1 / claim 17'), never by a [dddd] specification-paragraph bracket; [dddd] brackets are reserved for specification paragraphs. The Phase-1 invention-summary Quote-anchor table / quote-anchor-conventions should mark each Quotable span as claim-sourced vs spec-sourced so the convention is decided upstream and thesis-trace cannot inherit a spec bracket onto claim text. File at watch (first occurrence); a second occurrence promotes to recommended-apply.

root_cause_artifact: _shared/scripts/gate_figure_use.py (_figure_numbers reads the ENTIRE figure_selection_text, not just the selected-figure section)
finding: Phase-2 composer discovery (this run): gate_figure_use builds its 'selected' set by regex-scanning the WHOLE of figure-selection.md, including the '## Reviewed but NOT selected (with reason)' table and the '## Figure relationships' table. For run 045 that makes selected={1,2,3,4,5} when only FIG. 1/4/5 are actually selected (FIG. 2/3 are explicitly DROPPED). Intentionally-dropped figures therefore become FIGUSE-001 'orphans' unless the prose mentions them anyway. The defect did NOT fire this run only because the draft references FIG. 2/3 in prose (thesis-trace coverage note); a clean drop of the dropped figures from prose — the legitimate authoring intent — would have spuriously hard-FAILED goal-2 on figures 2 and 3. Mechanically verified against gate_figure_use.py: whole-file parse yields {1,2,3,4,5}; a section-scoped parse yields the correct {1,4,5}. Distinct mechanism from figure-token-regex-blindspot (that is lettered-panel tokens like 'FIG. 4B'); this is selected-set SCOPE over-read of the selection file. Mitigation cost (mention dropped figures in prose so they are not orphaned) recurs latently per run with a 'Reviewed but NOT selected' section.
recommendation: Gate-strengthening: scope the selected-set parse to the region between the '## Selected figures' heading and the next '## ' heading; fall back to whole-text when that heading is absent (legacy/bare-list compatibility, preserves all fixtures + test_gates SELECTION constant). Ship with a test_gates.py case. Proposal on file (2026-06-24-figuse-selection-scope.md), watch.

finding: The three-gap recap (functional legs [0143] / disclaimed autonomy [0135] / one hardware fence is a loading mechanism [0144]) appears in §3 (evidence), §4 ¶1 (verdict ledger), and §5 ¶1 (thesis landing). Three appearances, each a distinct arc beat, reads as deliberate reinforcement not padding. Flagged for awareness only; accepted as-is. Same class as prior runs' sanctioned-repetition-layering awareness flags.
recommendation: Optional: let §5 ¶1 assert the verdict at a higher level rather than re-listing all three at §4's granularity. Acceptable as-is.

finding: §5 ¶2 execution-series forward pointer (whole-body manipulation, in-house cycloidal gears/actuators, human-coexistence safety) rests on agility-execution-portfolio (tier-4, Contrary Research). Accepted as framed: explicit 'look here next' pointer the essay says it 'does not adjudicate', not load-bearing for a verdict, source IS in # Sources, hedged ('if the technical moat exists', 'marks where to look'). First-seen class: a tier-4 source used for a hedged, non-load-bearing forward pointer — within bounds, recorded for cross-run watch.
recommendation: No change required; keep the forward pointer hedged. Optional 'widely described as' qualifier to make the sourcing posture explicit. Watch whether tier-4-sourced forward pointers recur and whether any becomes load-bearing.

root_cause_artifact: essay-en-composer/references/section-blueprint.md (no paragraph word-length band for mobile rendering)
finding: Four paragraphs run ~10-11 mobile lines: §2 ¶1 and §3 ¶4 are verbatim-claim-quote + narrative + attribution units (quote-integrated demotion applies — length justified by holding a long legal quote intact); §4 ¶1 (~125w) and §4 ¶2 (~127w) are non-quote and sit at the TOP of the comfortable range. None reaches the 8-sentence ceiling; none is a comprehension break. Polish, not a density wall. Removing the [0144] brackets made §2 ¶1 / §3 ¶4 marginally shorter (no readability regression). Marginal recurrence of the cross-run mobile-paragraph-wall class (gate-invisible to STRUCT-001 sentence counting), but at the edge, not a wall in the prior-run medium sense.
recommendation: Optional, mobile only: split §4 ¶2 at the legal-vs-prior-art seam ('The second exposure is the one already on the record...'). Leave the two quote-bearing paragraphs intact. Same word-band heuristic the cross-run mobile-paragraph-wall proposal already covers.

finding: post-accept revision (v2.4): 'compressed section-3 was a 4-sentence stub between larger sections' -> 'merged section-3 + section-4 into "The Territory Is Not the Moat"'. a section far shorter than its siblings is a rhythm break; merge or expand.

finding: no findings (empty-pass marker: all 6 gates pass with zero findings on essay-draft.md and published essay-final.md - emdash, anchors, sources, banned, structure, figure_use). Formal pipeline run; inner loop PASS at iteration 1.

Latent rubric asymmetry, surfaced by the prior ad-hoc run on this patent and PREVENTED here. The ad-hoc run passed the loop yet closed over-defensively (led with 'qualified yes'; gave moat and limits equal weight via 'limits are equally real'; re-listed caveats already covered), caught only by a SETI read. The 6 passes gate factual OVERREACH (pass-3/pass-4) but no pass gates OVER-HEDGE (a verdict too defensive for its evidence). This run pinned a firm-closing posture in thesis-spine and drafted it from the first draft; pass-6 plus the SETI firm-closing guard confirmed it held. Proposal on file.
recommendation: Add a pass-6 sub-check 'verdict confidence proportionate to evidence' (flag leading-with-qualifier, thesis/limits false-equivalence, re-listed caveats) and/or a posture-lens note that investor/analysis editions default to a firmer close. See meta/improvement-proposals/2026-06-24-conclusion-over-hedge-check.md.

finding: no findings (claim-scope: the invention-summary claim-scope map locked/open/pinned was authored and honored; no dependent/description embodiment (safe-stop, kneel, ML model, payload, standing-to-non-standing) attributed to claim 1; pinned fall-extent values not described as bounds; all 14 [00NN] anchors byte-verified vs Quotable spans; external facts within fact-check-log tiers and fenced). The system's most damaging class, claim-scope-misattribution, did NOT recur this run with the map applied.
'$2.5 billion' appears in the lead (hook/stakes) and again in the market-context section (sourced detail); accepted lead->recap layering, not a third restatement. No action.
recommendation: Keep sanctioned repetition layers enumerated so revision does not add a new one unnoticed.

root_cause_artifact: essay-en-composer/references/x-articles-format-en.md (Sources Papers entry — venue when only patent-cited)
finding: The three academic papers are cited as listed in the patent's Other Publications (title + year, no venue). Verified Tier 2 (cited in the granted patent); venue not load-bearing. Adjacent to the prior runs' sources-entry-template-drift class.
recommendation: Optional: add venue if a primary lookup is budgeted; otherwise accept patent-citation provenance.

finding: Deliverable hard-wrapped at ~95 columns; X Articles honors single newlines, so mid-sentence wraps render ragged. Publication needs one line per paragraph. No format rule and no strip-pipeline normalization existed.
recommendation: x-articles-format-en line-wrap rule + strip-pipeline paragraph-rejoin step. See meta/improvement-proposals/2026-06-26-publication-line-wrap.md.

finding: gate_structure warns only at >8 sentences but editorial Pass 2C flags >=8; exactly-8-sentence paragraphs pass the gate and fail the editor. The ad-hoc run hit this 3x including a fix-introduced regression. Off-by-one between the mechanical and editorial layers.
recommendation: Set MAX_SENTENCES_PER_PARA=7 so STRUCT-001 warns at >=8, matching Pass 2C; add a test. See meta/improvement-proposals/2026-06-26-gate-structure-sentence-band-align.md.

root_cause_artifact: tools/ (no figure-sequence composition helper; cover spec 5:2 not surfaced at figure time)
finding: Building the FIG. 5 cover (ground-line alignment, uniform captions, even spacing, 5:2 aspect) was re-derived from scratch over many iterations. A PIL gotcha (Image.getbbox trims black not white; use ImageChops.difference against white) was a real time-sink. No tool or doc captures this.
recommendation: Build tools/compose_figure_sequence.py + docs/figure-composition.md (5:2 spec, baseline-align, uniform captions, white-trim gotcha) + a CLAUDE.md pointer. Retro L6.

root_cause_artifact: _shared/scripts/gate_structure.py (STRUCT-001 threshold off-by-one vs Pass 2C) + essay-en-composer/references/section-blueprint.md (no jointly-specified word+sentence paragraph band)
finding: Iter-1 sole HIGH and the driver of the iter-1 revise-required: the §4 'The kneel is the next example' paragraph ran 213 words across 8 sentences — over both the 3-7 sentence band (Pass 2C flags >=8 as high) and the mobile word ceiling. STRUCT-001 warns only at >8 sentences (n>=9), so the exactly-8-sentence wall passed the mechanical gate and was caught only by the editorial pass-5/pass-2C read, exactly the off-by-one the gate-structure-sentence-band-align proposal targets. [resolved across iter-2/iter-3: §4 split at grounding-neutral seams to P15 65w + P16 76w; final densest body paragraph 126w/6 sentences, no paragraph >130w or >7 sentences.] Same class as the 2026-06-26 investor-edition retro paragraph-eight-sentence-slip (the prior ad-hoc run on this same patent hit exactly-8 three times incl. a fix-introduced regression).
recommendation: Apply the on-file proposal 2026-06-26-gate-structure-sentence-band-align.md (set MAX_SENTENCES_PER_PARA=7 so STRUCT-001 warns at >=8, matching Pass 2C; warn-only, no verdict flip) so the 8-sentence wall surfaces mechanically at compose time; complemented by 2026-06-11-gate-structure-word-wall.md (STRUCT-005 word ceiling) for the word-count half of the same paragraph.

finding: Iter-1 claim-scope asymmetry: the essay pinned the rising-risk reconfiguration to claim 1 but left the 'safe operating stop' itself unpinned relative to the claim boundary, so a reader could not tell that the stop rung sits in dependent claim 2 + the description (paras 31, 42), NOT in claim 1, whose block quote closes at 'lowering a center-of-gravity'. The same locked/open/pinned class as the system's most damaging defect, but here surfaced at MEDIUM (not a full grounding breach) and caught inside the loop. [resolved in iter-2: the claim-2 safe-stop pin added explicitly ('the stop itself is dependent claim 2 plus the description, not part of claim 1'); re-verified intact in iter-3 against patent claims 1 and 2.] NOTE: the immediately prior formal run on this patent (2026-06-24-us12560948b2-safe-stop) authored and honored the claim-scope map and PREVENTED this asymmetry; this e2e run shows the residual as a caught-and-fixed MEDIUM rather than a prevented one.
recommendation: On-file proposal 2026-06-11-claim-scope-lock-map.md already covers this: the invention-summary Claim scope map must mark the safe-operating-stop as open relative to claim 1 (dependent claim 2 + description), so Compose pins it from the first draft rather than leaving the asymmetry for pass-3 to catch.

root_cause_artifact: essay-en-composer/references/section-blueprint.md (no paragraph word-length band for mobile rendering) + _shared/scripts/gate_structure.py (STRUCT-001 counts sentences, not words)
finding: Mobile paragraph-density cluster spanning iter-1 into iter-2: beyond the 213w §4 wall, dense word-count paragraphs at §1 'A wheeled cart freezes...' 150w, §4 'The kneel is the next example' 141w, and §3 'Conventional machine safety...' 130w render past the ~8 mobile-line ceiling while sitting under the 8-sentence count, so STRUCT-001 left them unflagged (gate-invisible by the same mechanism as runs 1-2). Carried as the iter-2 residual MEDIUM (densest then 150w §1 / 141w §4). [resolved iter-3: all three split at grounding-neutral seams -> 108w+42w, 65w+76w, 61w+69w; final densest body paragraph 126w, none >130w.] Exact recurrence of the cross-run mobile-paragraph-wall class.
recommendation: On-file proposal 2026-06-11-gate-structure-word-wall.md (recommended-apply, recurrence 4+): add STRUCT-005 word-count warn (~>110w) so these surface mechanically at compose time instead of costing iterations; the deferred section-blueprint companion (joint word+sentence band) addresses the revision-induced-band-break interaction.

finding: self-audit revision (self-audit-1): '"Its photons travel on into open space, so its return arrives late and weak ... One row reads much farther than the smooth pattern says it should ... The patent even names the early-warning signal ... the drop in \'the peak rate of the intensity of light captured by each row of zones 114 in the ROI 116\' as a row loses its ground return [0039]."' -> 'earliest/long-range warning now attributed to the top row\'s peak intensity falling below the room\'s ambient/background light [0039]; the "reads much farther"/loss-of-ground distance jump is presented as the closer-range signal.'. skeptical-engineer grounding finding (medium), verifiable against the patent — long-range trigger is peak-intensity-below-ambient ([0042],[0050],[0060]-[0066], claim 2); the distance jump is the short-range loss-of-ground ([0056],[0077],[0080]). The draft fused the two range mechanisms and labeled the distance signal as the early warning.
recommendation: captured via the revision-delta channel; owner artifact + lever per attribution-table.

finding: self-audit revision (self-audit-1): '"roughly a thirty-five-fold jump ... A thirty-five-fold finer grid is the difference between ..."' -> '"... No specification under-use.

finding: no findings
recommendation: Pass 6 clean: lead puts the downward-sensor limit [0004] on the table by sentence 5 with thesis tension framed; closing returns to the frame and lands on an open question matching spine residual_risk=Acknowledged; # Sources present exactly once as a single h1, three enum categories, fully subgrouped (all-or-nothing satisfied); no em-dash in title/body; all [dddd] cites 4-digit; no footnote defs leaked.

finding: The seeing-becomes-doing thesis is asserted in §3 ('Seeing becomes doing') and re-landed in §5 ('turn what it sees into what the robot does'). This is the acceptable lead-anchor -> closing-recap pattern (two sections, distinct framing each time: §3 closes the claim beat, §5 generalizes it), not 3+-location inflation. No numeric value or patent claim is restated 3+ times without a new evidence layer. Flagged only for awareness; no action required.
recommendation: Optional / none. Keep both; they bookend the arc rather than repeat it.

finding: §3 'That comparison is the whole invention' slightly over-narrows: the same paragraph and the [0005] anchor make clear the invention also requires the propulsion change ('changing a propulsion ... before reaching the edge'). The essay self-corrects two paragraphs later ('This claim does not end at a cliff is present. It ends in motion'), so the reader is not misled, but the flat 'whole invention' reads as rhetorical emphasis on one half of a two-step (seeing + doing) claim. LOW and self-corrected (not the grounding-breach magnitude of the system's most damaging claim-scope class) — a rhetorical over-narrowing of a two-step claim to its first step.
recommendation: Optional. Soften to 'That comparison is the heart of the invention' or 'is what the claim turns on', reserving 'whole' for the seeing-plus-doing pair the §3 close names.

finding: §5 sentence beginning 'The module behind this class of sensor resolves 2,268 zones' runs ~50 words (gate counts 60) and stacks two source parentheticals ('(VL53L9CX zone resolution) ... (VL53L9CX range and frame rate)') inside a single span carrying the 2,268-zone count, the 35x comparison, the 9 m range and the 100 fps. One of the genuine LONGSENT warn-gate hits. Meaning is intact and the claim cluster is legitimate (a necessary spec citation), but on a mobile column it renders as a dense run-on right before the closing. Under conservative posture this would escalate toward medium; under measured it is polish-level. Same §5 sentence as the gate's LONGSENT-001 60-word warn — sentence-length run-on, distinct from the word-per-PARAGRAPH mobile-paragraph-wall class.
recommendation: Optional. Split after '(VL53L9CX zone resolution)' into two sentences so the resolution leap lands, then the range/frame-rate as a short follow-on. No content change needed.

finding: Read as the impatient reader: §1 paragraph 1 ends on a question ('how a machine ... keeps catching a hole in the floor') rather than a declarative verdict, so strict BLUF would call the verdict deferred to §3. This is the essay's sanctioned technical-impossibility hook for a high-school-to-undergrad explainer (an everyday scene that raises the reader's own first-order objection), gate_meta passed, and the verdict arrives cleanly at §3. Remaining pass-7 checks clean: every ## header is an assertion and a header-only skim reconstructs the argument; the strongest pro-subject counter is conceded then refined in §3 (steelman present); no reader-instruction/self-reference meta; jargon kept at signpost depth; no stub section (sections ~165-372 words, balanced); core verdict asserted in <=3 sections.
recommendation: Optional. The question-lead is defensible for this audience and posture; if a harder BLUF is wanted, add one declarative clause at the end of para 1 naming the answer ('forward-looking sensing'). Not required for pass.

finding: gate_structure STRUCT-004 (warn): 3 'A, B, and C' triad(s) detected (first at line 14). Warn-only heuristic behaving as designed — the triads are factual/legitimate enumerations (e.g. the senses/bridge/nervous-system metaphor, claim-6 object-detection/wall-tracking/SLAM list), not AI rule-of-three tics. No action.
recommendation: None. Warn-only, behaving as designed; the triads are factual enumerations not rhetorical padding.

finding: gate_typography LONGSENT-001 (warn x8): sentences run 70/49/36/41/53/60/50/57 words (target ~15-25). The 70-word hit is the frontmatter/YAML header line (parser artifact, not prose) and the 57-word hit is a Sources-block URL line (Raynor. https://patents.google.com/... — also not running prose); the genuine prose run-ons are the 49/36/41/53/60/50-word body sentences, the densest being the §5 60-word 'module behind this class of sensor resolves 2,268 zones' sentence (same span as the pass-5 reader-perspective LOW). Warn-only, no verdict flip. Recurring per-run mobile-readability warn-cluster: each run trips several LONGSENT warns that pass mechanically but cost a polish-level editorial note.
recommendation: Two sub-issues: (1) the frontmatter-header and Sources-URL false hits suggest LONGSENT should skip YAML frontmatter + Sources-block lines (gate scope refinement); (2) the genuine prose run-ons recur every run as warn-only polish notes — candidate for a section-blueprint sentence-length band so Compose self-limits before the gate. Watch until the class recurs with a body-sentence count.

root_cause_artifact: _shared/scripts/gate_figure_use.py (_figure_numbers / FIG_RE reads the ENTIRE figure_selection_text, not just the '## Selected figures' section)
finding: SECOND confirmed occurrence (first: run 045). This run is TEXT-ONLY: zero figures selected (input/figures/ empty, '## Selected figures' table = (none)). But figure-selection.md still discusses the patent drawings in a '## Paired-figure relationships (acknowledged)' table + explanatory prose for documentation. Because gate_figure_use regex-scans the WHOLE file for FIG.N/Figure-N tokens to build the 'selected' set, any literal figure token in those reference-only sections would false-count as SELECTED and emit a spurious FIGUSE-001 orphan hard-FAIL on a text-only essay. The gate passed THIS run only because the Phase-1 author defensively wrote every figure reference in WORDS ('the robot/architecture group', 'four-panel approach sequence') and added an explicit warning note ('gate_figure_use parses every FIG.N token in this document as selected, so the reference-only rows below describe the patent drawings in words to avoid a spurious orphan FAIL ... the gate should parse only the Selected figures table'). That author-side workaround IS the recurring per-run mitigation cost. Mechanically verified: current whole-file parse + a future natural-token relationships table -> spurious selected set -> FIGUSE-001 hard FAIL with zero figures actually selected. This is the confirmed real gate bug that recurs on every text-only / few-figure run with a relationships or reviewed-but-not-selected section.
recommendation: Gate-strengthening: scope the selected-set parse to the region between the '## Selected figures' heading and the next '## ' heading; fall back to whole-text when that heading is absent (legacy/bare-list compat, preserves all fixtures + test_gates SELECTION constant). Second occurrence promotes the on-file watch proposal to recommended-apply: see 2026-06-30-figuse-selected-section-scope-recommend.md. Must pass meta/regression.py before a human applies.
